stimulus: In his novel sequence The Border Trilogy, Cormac McCarthy weaves the lives of his young protagonists into larger currents of twentieth-century North American history. He frames John Grady Cole's 1949 ride into Mexico against the slow collapse of the cattle-ranching economy of the American Southwest. ______blank McCarthy positions Billy Parham's later wanderings against the shifting boundary politics and modernization of the postwar borderlands.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. "In the same way" logically signals that the second sentence is a parallel example to the first: both illustrate how McCarthy ties his protagonists' personal stories to broader historical forces in the borderlands. The two sentences agree, with the second offering a similar instance of the same pattern.

Choice A is incorrect because "by contrast" would signal opposition, yet the two sentences describe analogous cases. Choice C is incorrect because "therefore" would signal cause and effect, but Billy's storyline is not a consequence of John Grady's. Choice D is incorrect because "in particular" would mark the second sentence as a more specific instance of the first; instead, it offers a separate, parallel example.
